task_completion.description: Open and read the lab measurements file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/lab_measurements.txt'.

task_completion.description: Access the CAMEO Chemicals website to search for candidate chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to 'cameochemicals.noaa.gov' showing search results or chemical data pages for the candidate chemicals.

task_completion.description: Create the forensic identification report file in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with a file named 'chemical_identification_report.txt' located in the '~/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Correctly identify Bottle A and Bottle B with their reference Boiling Point (BP) and Flash Point (FP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report contains 'Bottle A: Acetonitrile (Ref BP: 82, Ref FP: 6)' and 'Bottle B: Tetrahydrofuran (Ref BP: 66, Ref FP: -14)'. Accept slight variations in spacing or inclusion of 'C' for Celsius.

task_completion.description: Correctly identify Bottle C and Bottle D with their reference Boiling Point (BP) and Flash Point (FP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report contains 'Bottle C: Pyridine (Ref BP: 115, Ref FP: 20)' and 'Bottle D: Ethyl Acetate (Ref BP: 77, Ref FP: -4)'. Accept slight variations in spacing or inclusion of 'C' for Celsius.

task_completion.description: Correctly identify Bottle E and ensure the exact required header format is used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report contains 'Bottle E: Cyclohexane (Ref BP: 81, Ref FP: -20)' and starts with the exact header 'FORENSIC IDENTIFICATION REPORT' followed by '==============================' on the next line.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with CAMEO Chemicals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent actively navigating, searching, and reading data on the CAMEO Chemicals website in a GUI browser, rather than using curl/wget or hallucinating the data.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to create the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing or pasting the report into a GUI text editor (like gedit, nano, or VS Code) rather than generating the entire file instantly via a pre-written script or single terminal command.

integrity.description: The agent actually viewed the lab measurements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent opening or cat-ing 'lab_measurements.txt' to read the data before making the matches.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/chemical_identification_report.txt'. The correct matches and reference properties (in Celsius) are: Bottle A: Acetonitrile (BP: 82, FP: 6); Bottle B: Tetrahydrofuran (BP: 66, FP: -14); Bottle C: Pyridine (BP: 115, FP: 20); Bottle D: Ethyl Acetate (BP: 77, FP: -4); Bottle E: Cyclohexane (BP: 81, FP: -20). The VLM should look for these exact values in the final report.
